item.title: Location
item.description: Address and location for Professor Ethan Ligon.

item.title: Office Hours
item.description: Schedule and location for Professor Ethan Ligon's office hours.

item.title: No More Grading: Incentive Compatible Peer Assessment in a Project-Based Course
item.description: Describes an incentive-compatible system for grading collaborative student work using peer assessment, SVD-based score extraction, and strategic team formation.

item.title: Consumer Demand with Price Aggregators and Low-Rank Cross-Price Effects
item.description: Characterizes preferences that rationalize demand systems with price aggregators, providing flexible and tractable forms for estimating consumer demand with many goods.

item.title: Credit and Welfare Across the Lean Season
item.description: Measures demand for credit across the lean season in Nigeria.

item.title: sucoder
item.description: Sandbox for AI coding agents using Unix filesystem permissions---treating the agent as a collaborator with its own account.

item.title: Risk-Sharing Tests and Covariate Shocks: Drought, Floods, and Pests in Uganda
item.description: Devises new methods to compute households' marginal utilities of expenditure based on nonhomothetic preferences. This allows us to test efficient risk-sharing even when shocks are covariate.

item.title: LSMS Library
item.description: A Python library providing uniform access to Living Standards Measurement Study household survey data across countries and years.

item.title: Assessing Targeting Performance: The Case of Ghana's LEAP Program
item.description: Evaluates targeting accuracy of Ghana's LEAP social protection program using marginal utility of expenditure as a welfare benchmark.

item.title: Spatial Procurement of Farm Products and the Supply of Processed Foods
item.description: Develops a spatial model of farm product procurement by food processors, applied to California's tomato processing industry.

item.title: Consumption Subaggregates Should Not Be Used to Measure Poverty
item.description: Shows that using consumption subaggregates to measure poverty is theoretically unjustified and empirically misleading.

item.title: Structural Experimentation to Distinguish between Models of Risk Sharing with Frictions in Rural Paraguay
item.description: Uses dictator-type games in rural Paraguay to distinguish between models of risk sharing with limited commitment, moral hazard, and hidden income.

item.title: Inferring Informal Risk-Sharing Regimes: Evidence from Rural Tanzania
item.description: Exploits the link between interest rates and consumption moments to test alternative risk-sharing models without interest rate data.

item.title: What Explains Low Adoption of Digital Payment Technologies?
item.description: Investigates why small-scale merchants in Jaipur, India have low adoption rates for digital payment technologies despite minimal supply-side barriers.

item.title: Estimating the Relative Benefits of Agricultural Growth on the Distribution of Expenditures
item.description: Estimates the relative benefits of agricultural versus non-agricultural GDP growth on household expenditures across the income distribution.

item.title: All -Separable Demands and Rationalizing Utility Functions
item.description: Completely characterizes all Frisch demand systems that are separable in the budget-constraint multiplier, and the utility functions that rationalize them.

item.title: Some -Separable Frisch Demands with Utility Functions
item.description: Provides closed-form utility functions for several families of lambda-separable Frisch demands used in applied work.

item.title: Motives for Sharing in Social Networks
item.description: Uses variants of dictator games to decompose motives for sharing in Paraguayan villages into directed altruism, reciprocity, and sanctions.

item.title: Incentives & Nutrition for Rotten Kids: The Quantity & Quality of Food Allocated within Philippine Households
item.description: Distinguishes the quantity and quality of food allocated within households when nutrition affects both utility and future productivity, and tests the model with Philippine data.

item.title: Nutrition and Risk Sharing within the Household
item.description: Tests risk-sharing within households by contrasting the unitary model with one in which nutrition affects future productivity, using individual consumption data from Philippine farm households.

item.title: Risk Management in the Cooperative Contract
item.description: Shows how agricultural cooperatives can use multi-year relationships to provide insurance against crop shortfalls.

item.title: Agricultural Contracts: Data and Research Needs
item.description: Surveys the landscape of agricultural contracting and identifies data collection priorities for advancing research on contracts in agriculture.

item.title: Poverty and the Welfare Costs of Risk Associated with Globalization
item.description: Uses 50 years of Lorenz curve data to measure the welfare costs of risk borne by poor households and whether globalization increases that risk.

item.title: Formal Markets and Informal Insurance
item.description: Examines how the introduction of formal credit markets may undermine informal insurance arrangements in developing-country villages.

item.title: Using Production Data to Design Efficient Contracts
item.description: Shows how production data can be used to construct optimal contracts for agricultural commodities, applied to California processing tomatoes.

item.title: Measuring Vulnerability
item.description: Constructs a measure of vulnerability that decomposes welfare losses into components due to poverty, aggregate risk, and idiosyncratic risk.

item.title: Economic Distance and Cross-Country Spillovers
item.description: Estimates the magnitude of cross-country growth spillovers using a novel measure of economic distance between nations.

item.title: Estimation of an Efficient Tomato Contract
item.description: Estimates an agency model of processing tomato contracts, measuring grower risk aversion and the efficiency of existing quality measurement.

item.title: Dynamic Bargaining in Households (with an Application to Bangladesh)
item.description: Uses a dynamic limited-commitment framework to model intra-household bargaining, replacing the Pareto-optimality axiom of Nash Bargaining with a weaker efficiency notion.

item.title: Informal Insurance Arrangements with Limited Commitment
item.description: Characterises efficient informal insurance arrangements constrained by limited commitment, and tests the model using data from three Indian villages.

item.title: Optimal Risk in Agricultural Contracts
item.description: Characterizes the optimal level of risk in agricultural production contracts, showing that "less risk" need not mean "smaller variance."

item.title: Policing Mechanisms in Agricultural Contracts
item.description: Identifies four policing instruments in California fruit and vegetable contracts: input control, monitoring, quality measurement, and revenue sharing.

item.title: Agricultural Markets as Relative Performance Evaluation
item.description: Shows that agricultural spot markets can function as relative performance evaluation, using market prices to infer grower effort.

item.title: Mutual Insurance, Individual Savings, and Limited Commitment
item.description: Examines how the availability of individual savings interacts with informal mutual insurance when commitment is limited.

item.title: Incentive Instruments in Fruit and Vegetable Contracts
item.description: Identifies four generic incentive instruments used in California fruit and vegetable contracts to coordinate grower-handler relationships.

item.title: Agricultural Supply Response Under Contract
item.description: Analyzes how contracts with risk-neutral intermediaries change the supply response of risk-averse agricultural producers.

item.title: Producer Price Risk and Quality Measurement
item.description: Shows that imperfect quality measurement creates a moral hazard problem in which efficient contracts expose growers to residual price risk.

item.title: Government Management of Village Commons
item.description: Compares two government policies for managing village forest commons when direct enforcement is difficult.

item.title: Liquidity Constraints and Incentive Contracts
item.description: Develops methods to distinguish whether firms and households face exogenous credit constraints or endogenous constraints arising from moral hazard or limited commitment.

item.title: Risk-Sharing and Information in Village Economies
item.description: Tests the implications of private information for risk sharing in ICRISAT villages, finding that information problems constrain consumption smoothing.

item.title: Computing the Equilibria of Dynamic Common Property Games
item.description: Develops computational methods for finding equilibria of dynamic games in which multiple agents exploit a common-pool resource.

item.title: The Swamp and the Shopping Center: An Interest Rate Parable
item.description: Uses a parable of a swamp and a shopping center to illustrate how the choice of discount rate affects cost-benefit analysis of environmental projects.
